eyebrow: Turn an unpolished message into shared working context
headline: Message Rustam, get a brief others can use.
subheadline: Write the messy version. Rustam will return five lines: situation, desired change, binding constraint, open unknown, and first check.
chatStarter: Rustam, I need to explain why this AI handoff fails without forwarding our entire chat history.
proofLine: Start with a free 15-minute fit check.
whyItWorks: A fixed five-line artifact makes clarification portable across clients, teammates, and developers.

eyebrow: Turn implementation warnings into accountable client action
headline: Send me the caveat, assign its owner.
subheadline: Paste the warning unchanged. I'll label verified fact, unresolved condition, client-owned choice, and the person accountable for follow-through.
chatStarter: Rustam, my developer says this is risky, but I cannot tell who must choose or act.
whyItWorks: Four labels expose where engineering ends and client responsibility begins.

eyebrow: Make competing explanations predict different observations
headline: Chat with Rustam, find evidence that separates causes.
subheadline: Share two examples and two counterexamples. Rustam will build competing prediction columns, then choose the cheapest observation on which they disagree.
chatStarter: Rustam, the sync fails unpredictably, and every explanation seems plausible after the fact.
whyItWorks: Prediction columns turn vague causes into an affordable observation that can reject a cause.

eyebrow: Let the problem earn ongoing support
headline: Chat with me, pay only when continuity helps.
subheadline: Use the free fit check. I'll score continuity on retained context, follow-up evidence, and recurring judgment; without those, I'll recommend a concise answer.
chatStarter: Rustam, I need help now, but I do not know whether value continues after the first answer.
proofLine: Start with a free 15-minute fit check.
whyItWorks: Three continuity tests make monthly support conditional on value the first answer cannot provide.

eyebrow: Every answer should survive beyond its original case
headline: Rustam's replies build judgment you reuse alone.
subheadline: Each reply ends with a rule, its exception, and a transfer question you apply to a different case without Rustam.
chatStarter: Rustam, help me choose this workflow, then show me how to judge a similar choice independently.
whyItWorks: Rule, exception, and transfer question convert advice into independent capability.

headline: Message Rustam, test whether clarity earns support.
subheadline: After the free chat, test three outputs: a sharper problem, a named evidence target, and a boundary that removes irrelevant work.
chatStarter: Rustam, I want to see whether our conversation materially improves this problem before I choose ongoing help.
proofLine: Start with a free 15-minute fit check.
whyItWorks: Three visible outputs make fit observable instead of relying on rapport or reassurance.

eyebrow: Ongoing value should leave visible evidence
headline: Ask Rustam, define proof that earns the month.
subheadline: Rustam will set a four-line ledger: monthly question, changed action, avoided rework, and review date. Empty lines trigger a scope reset.
chatStarter: Rustam, what would we record each month to know whether your support is changing my work?
whyItWorks: An explicit ledger ties recurring fees to changed work and prompts correction when value disappears.

eyebrow: Compare the polished story with the sentence you withheld
headline: Send me the awkward line, uncover its cost.
subheadline: Paste the polished version beside the awkward line. I'll mark the changed actor, hidden consequence, and constraint the official story omitted.
chatStarter: Officially, adoption is slow. Awkwardly, our team does not trust the results enough to show clients.
whyItWorks: Side-by-side wording reveals which consequence disappears when the situation gets polished.

eyebrow: Protect what the customer must still receive
headline: Message Rustam, protect the deadline with a fallback.
subheadline: Name the immovable date. Rustam will define the minimum customer promise, latest safe switch time, fallback delivery, and notification line.
chatStarter: Rustam, the launch date stays, but the integration may fail and customers still need a usable result.
whyItWorks: Four fallback elements protect the customer promise when the preferred route becomes unsafe.

eyebrow: Candor should include a path to revision
headline: Get testable disagreement in Rustam's direct reply.
subheadline: Rustam will steelman your preferred option, name its weakest assumption, then set rescue evidence and a stop condition you can observe.
chatStarter: Rustam, challenge my plan fairly and show what evidence would make you withdraw the objection.
proofLine: Recommendations include assumptions, evidence, and conditions for revision.
whyItWorks: Steelman, falsifier, and stop condition make disagreement useful without turning it into authority.
